videos.hook_text: Flip a coin 10 times in a row -- what are the odds you get ALL heads?
videos.notes: First ledger entry for this slug -- no prior analytics to retrieve, so bet is pure variety: every segment gets its own new mechanic (narrowing gates, dice tower, shrinking card fan, growth line) instead of repeating the bar/bubble kit defaults that were previously on 2 of 4 segments.

timeline.detail: coin flip curiosity question over narrowing-odds-gates build-in
dice-probability-tower: sum-7 tower rises tall vs matching short 2/12 towers
shrinking-deck-fan: card fan loses a card, target card glows, odds tick up
growth-line-photo: seedling rides the rising application-index line
closing line: luck was math nobody calculated
comment-the-number question

videos.hook_text: Somewhere right now, a language is taking its last breath -- and it happens every 14 days.
videos.notes: Fixed a real data bug while authoring: language_distribution's Mandarin point was 1.3 (should be 1300, i.e. 1.3 billion) which made the top language render as the smallest bar -- corrected the unit and added the missing Hindi/Arabic points so the say line's 5-language claim actually matches the data. Kept exactly one photo+number layout (the flag ground-row) per house variety rule; the other 3 segments are fresh procedural mechanics.

timeline.detail: a language dies every 14 days, over urgency-dials build-in
flag ground-row: Mandarin/Spanish/English/Hindi/Arabic sized by native speakers
digital-silence-globe: globe lit only 10% up, dark zone dotted with fading languages
seedling-revival-meter: revival dial + sprouting leaves for the 15%/yr learner growth
closing line: half of today's 7,000 languages could go silent
what-can-we-do question

videos.hook_text: Right now, giant slabs of Earth are racing each other -- but which one is actually winning?
videos.notes: No prior nature/geology videos in the ledger to compare against yet -- clean slate, so the bet is maximum variety: all 4 segments got brand-new procedural mechanics (a splitting-globe race, a widening fault chasm, a thickening drift trail across millions of years, twin fill gauges for the boundary effects) instead of the object/stack/timeline_axis/fill_object kit placeholders the config previously had, and every say line was rewritten to speak the exact cm/yr and percent figures each mechanic shows.

timeline.detail: which plate is winning the race -- over plate-drift-race build-in
fault-chasm-split: ground splits open, Arabian plate's 5 cm/yr gap widens fastest vs Pacific's 3
drift-trail-timeline: trail thickens from 2 cm/yr (10M years ago) to 3 cm/yr (today)
twin-effect-gauges: volcano gauge fills to 80%, earthquake gauge to 90%
closing line: the map you know today won't exist in 10 million years
fault line vs volcano -- which would scare you more question

videos.hook_text: Two of the world's most famous volcanoes are pouring out lava right now -- but one is erupting twice as fast. Which one?
videos.notes: No prior nature/geology videos in the ledger yet either -- authored back-to-back with tectonic-plates-on-the-move so the two share the 'nature' bucket but deliberately zero overlapping mechanics between them. All 4 segments got new procedural mechanics (a lava-river reach race, twin filling magma shafts, a growing eruption plume across a year timeline, two islands' crust creeping up from the sea) replacing the object/stack/timeline_axis/fill_object placeholders, with say lines rewritten to speak the exact m3/s and km3 figures.

timeline.detail: which volcano erupts twice as fast -- over lava-river-race build-in
molten-shaft-fill: two magma shafts fill to Kilauea's 1 km3 vs Etna's 0.5 km3 totals
eruption-plume-timeline: plume grows from 50 m3/s (2018) to 100 m3/s (2020)
island-rising-from-sea: Surtsey/Hawaii crust creeps up to 20%/30% lava coverage
closing line: Surtsey was born entirely from lava like this
fast lava you can outrun vs slow lava that never stops -- question
